notFound.title: Looks like you're lost.
notFound.body: This page doesn't exist. The link may be outdated or a project may have been renamed. Pick one of the options below to continue.

nav.tooltipDocs: The .md files in your vault — add `kind:` to frontmatter and they become ontology nodes
nav.tooltipOntology: Hierarchical view of frontmatter nodes (project → domain → capability → element)
nav.tooltipTopology: Project dependency map — one of the ontology's exit views

modeBadge.vaultTooltip: Vault mode — {name} ({count} docs). Every change is saved to your local disk.
modeBadge.demoAriaLabelClickable: Demo mode — click to open my markdown folder
modeBadge.demoTooltip: Demo mode — no vault picked. Build-time demo manifest only; changes are not saved.
modeBadge.demoTooltipClickable: Demo mode — click to open my markdown folder (writeable mode)

landing.subtitle: Map your codebase — projects, features, who-depends-on-what — as plain markdown files. Browse it as a tree or a graph; ask AI agents to update it. Like Obsidian or Notion: pick a folder and start.
landing.topologyCaption: TOPOLOGY · {nodes, plural, one {# NODE} other {# NODES}} · {relations, plural, one {# RELATION} other {# RELATIONS}}
landing.step1Title: Write a markdown file per piece
landing.step1Body: One file per project / feature / module. Add a name and a few links in the header — that's a node, those are relations. AI agents (Claude Code, etc.) read and edit the same files.
landing.step2Body: "Who depends on this feature?" "What breaks if I change it?" — the answer is one click away instead of a search across files.
landing.step3Body: Browse as a tree, navigate as a graph, edit on a canvas. Distribution charts, dependency chains, hub nodes — all from the same folder.
landing.privacyNote: Local folders stay on your disk and are never sent anywhere. Your data, your truth source.

sigma.ariaLabel: Project topology map — jump to individual projects from the hub bar on the left

controls.searchCountAriaLabel: {visible} / {total, plural, one {# node} other {# nodes}} shown

tree.searchPlaceholder: Filter the tree — by title or slug

ontologyWidgets.manualSourceChipTitle: Node authored directly by a person or AI agent (vault frontmatter / builder / MCP)

globalSearch.dialogDescription: Search concepts (ontology nodes), documents, and projects in one place. Use up/down arrows to move, Enter to select, ESC to close.
globalSearch.placeholderWithProjects: Search concepts and projects — KR/EN mix OK
globalSearch.emptyNoCorpus: Nothing indexed yet. Create your first node in vault frontmatter or the builder and it will appear here.

projectSearch.helpRecent: Showing recently updated projects first. Narrow down by name, tags, or stack.
projectSearch.helpKeyboard: Use up/down arrows to move through results and Enter to select. ESC to close.
projectSearch.emptyNoProjectsTitle: No projects in this space yet.
projectSearch.emptyNoProjectsBody: Once projects are added, you can search and jump straight to them here.
projectSearch.emptyLayerBody: No projects match the selected “{layer}”. Reset the filter to see the full list again.
projectSearch.emptyNoMatchBody: Couldn't find “{query}” in any name, tag, or stack. Try a different word or clear the search to return to the full list.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.subtitle: Navigate the map without a mouse.
shortcuts.help: List of keyboard shortcuts available for navigation.

rows.openGlobalPalette: Search ontology, docs, and projects together
rows.toggleDocsDrawer: Toggle the docs vault preview drawer (with edit access)
rows.stepCloseOverlays: Close drawers and overlays one step at a time
rows.doubleClickLocal: Show only neighbors of the selected node (local graph)
rows.shiftClickPath: Highlight the shortest path between two nodes
rows.tabNeighbor: Move to a neighbor of the selected node
rows.queryCommandPrefix: Prefix the query for command mode
rows.queryTagPrefix: Prefix the query for tag mode
rows.scrollHeading: TOC sidebar follows the current heading
rows.hoverNeighbor: Highlight 1-hop neighbors and dim the rest
rows.togglePillView: Switch view via the top pill
rows.localVault: Use a local folder as your vault (File System Access)
rows.focusRefresh: Auto-refresh the local vault on tab focus
rows.pinDoc: Pin / unpin (pinned section stays on top)
rows.copyDocUrl: Copy current document URL to clipboard
rows.tagFilter: Click a tag chip to filter the tree
rows.modeToggle: Mode toggle — show only that mode's docs

hero.summaryDefault: Reading the connection structure that grows from your documents.
hero.docsVaultAriaLabel: Open docs vault — browse local folders and server docs in one place
hero.ontologyAriaLabel: Open ontology tree — view nodes and relations from vault frontmatter as a hierarchy

workspaceStrip.ontologyTitle: Sum of ontology nodes owned by this project — excludes project / vault-readme nodes. May differ from the 'N nodes' count on /ontology.
workspaceStrip.stubAriaLabel: Unresolved references — promote or discard from the tree
workspaceStrip.stubTitle: Nodes referenced via relates.target in frontmatter that don't exist yet. Promote or discard from the stub list at the bottom of /ontology.

gestureHint.body: Two-finger pinch to zoom, drag to pan. Tap a node to open details.

docsDrawer.totalLine: View all {count} registered documents at a glance.
docsDrawer.openAllAriaLabel: Open the full document vault page
docsDrawer.filterPlaceholder: Quick filter by title or path…
docsDrawer.filterAriaLabel: Filter documents by title or path

projectDrawer.openDocsVaultTitleWithDoc: Open the top related document for {name}
projectDrawer.summaryStandalone: Currently reads as a standalone project.

editor.discardConfirm: Discard unsaved changes and close the editor?

depsBar.notRegistered: This document isn't registered in Folder-Topology — move it to the projects/ directory and fill `name` · `category` in frontmatter to make it appear as a node.
depsBar.circularConfirm:
Warning: {dep} already depends on {current}.
Adding this dependency creates a cycle. Continue?

empty.selectPrompt: Pick a document from the document tree

outline.editTooltip: Edit this file (saves directly to the local vault)
outline.copyTooltip: Copy URL to this document to clipboard
outline.printTooltip: Open browser print dialog (save as PDF)
outline.deleteTooltip: Delete this document from the local vault

page.title: Ontology Builder
page.helpIntro: A workspace where you draw your knowledge graph by hand.
page.helpStepPalette: Click a kind from the palette on the left to add a new node
page.helpStepConnect: Drag handle → drop on target. Vault↔vault relations save automatically. If either node is unsaved (amber), click the Save chip on the edge.
page.helpStepEphemeral: Ephemeral nodes / edges have amber dashed styling — name the node in the inspector, then click Save on the edge to commit both at once.
page.helpShortcuts: P · project / D · domain / C · capability / E · element  ·  Del · delete selected  ·  Esc · clear  ·  F · fullscreen
page.exportAriaLabel: Export current canvas as frontmatter md
page.exportJsonLdAriaLabel: Export current canvas as JSON-LD (RDF 1.1 compatible)
page.exportGraphMlAriaLabel: Export current canvas as GraphML (Gephi / Cytoscape)
page.clearAriaLabel: Clear {nodes} ephemeral nodes and {edges} ephemeral relations
page.layoutDagreTitle: Kind hierarchy LR (project → domain → capability → element)
page.autoLayoutTooltip: Reset every node's position to the auto-layout result (frontmatter canvasPosition is preserved — your coordinates return on next visit)
page.mobileTitle: The builder shines on a bigger screen
page.mobileBody: It's a 3-pane ERD layout (palette · canvas · inspector), so the columns overlap on mobile. Open it again on desktop or tablet landscape. Meanwhile you can browse the ontology in tree or topology view.
page.toastDemoMode: Demo mode — can't save. Open a markdown folder under /docs and try again.
page.toastVaultConflict: This vault file was changed first by another editor or AI agent. Refresh and save again.
page.toastVaultEdgeDemo: Demo mode — open a vault folder from /docs to add relations.
page.toastEdgePersistNeedsTitle: Name the unsaved node first, then click Save on the edge.

blastRadius.titleWithBacklinks: {count} nodes depend on this — delete anyway?
blastRadius.bodyClean: The node and its frontmatter will be removed from the vault. This cannot be undone.
blastRadius.bodyWithBacklinks: These nodes reference the one you're about to delete. Their links will become dangling — you can fix them after, but plan a cleanup pass.
blastRadius.hintClean: Tip: builder edits write straight to disk. Re-create the node any time.
blastRadius.hintWithBacklinks: Tip: open the listed nodes after delete to remove the dangling reference, or rename instead of deleting.

canvas.ephemeralEdgeSaveAria: Save this relation to the vault
canvas.ephemeralEdgeSaveTooltip: Save this relation. Unsaved nodes are saved first.
canvas.emptyHint: Click a kind in the left palette to create your first node.

inspector.subtitle: Selected node detail and inline edits.
inspector.emptyHint: Click a kind in the left palette to create your first node, or click any canvas node to see its detail here.
inspector.saveButtonAriaLabel: Save this node to your vault folder as a .md file
inspector.ephemeralFooterEmpty: Enter a name to save. (Press Enter to save instantly)
inspector.ephemeralFooterReady: Press Enter to save — written as a markdown (.md) file.
inspector.vaultFooterReadOnly: Sample data — to edit, click [Demo →] above to open your markdown folder.
inspector.vaultFooterEditable: Only the name is reflected to the .md file. Body and other fields stay untouched. AI agents see the same folder and reflect changes immediately.
inspector.literalAutoSaveClean: Saving with an empty value removes the field entirely.

onboarding.title: Map your codebase together
onboarding.intro: A visual map of *features, relationships, and roles*. Draw it on the canvas — your AI agent (Claude Code, Cursor, …) reads and writes the same `.md` over MCP.
onboarding.stepPaletteStrong: Click a kind on the left (or press P/D/C/E)
onboarding.stepPaletteBody: — Project · Domain · Capability · Element. Hit the matching key to drop one without leaving the canvas.
onboarding.stepConnectStrong: Drag from a node's edge handle
onboarding.stepConnectBody: and drop on another node — the two get a *relation (depends / contains / describes)* between them. Vault↔vault relations save automatically. If either endpoint is unsaved (amber), click the **Save** chip on the edge to persist both at once.
onboarding.stepSaveBody: — straight to disk as `.md` when a vault is open. Without a vault the canvas is read-only (open one from the top right). AI agents read and write the same map over MCP.
onboarding.shortcutsHint: Quick keys — P/D/C/E new node · Del delete · Esc cancel

palette.subtitle: Pick a kind and click — a new node lands in the middle of the canvas.
palette.footerHint: Draft node → name in inspector + Enter to save.
palette.kindProjectHint: Top-level — one project in the whole workspace
palette.kindDomainHint: A big area inside a project — auth / billing / notifications, …
palette.kindCapabilityHint: One feature inside a domain — verb-style like "sign in"
palette.kindElementHint: A small piece inside a capability — screen / button / API

insights.title: Insights
insights.subtitle: Activity and structure stats for the whole ontology. The tree shows hierarchy; insights show where the hubs are and what grew most recently.
insights.emptyTitleBefore: Your ontology is empty. Start from the
insights.projectPanelSubtitle: {count} projects total — domain · capability · element sums, sorted by size
insights.edgeTypePanelSubtitle: {count} relations total — split by semantic relation type
insights.orphansPanelSubtitle: {count} nodes not hanging from the tree
insights.orphansEmpty: Every node is connected to the tree.

selector.emptyDescCanCreate: Create your first project to get going.
selector.quickCreateSubmit: Create and write the first doc
selector.newProjectDesc: Quickly add a new project from the bottom of the list and jump to its first doc.
selector.cardDescriptionFallback: This project doesn't have a description yet.

detail.stateInvalidDesc: No valid project slug, so detail can't be displayed.
detail.inlineDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this project in one line
detail.depCardDescription: Pick the hubs and services this project relies on; the topology updates instantly. Cycle-causing candidates are disabled.
detail.neighborMapDescription: A small map containing only the projects directly linked to this one. Drag, hover, and click behave just like the main topology.
detail.neighborMapEmpty: No directly linked projects yet. Add a slug under `dependencies:` in this project's frontmatter and the neighbors will appear here.
detail.infoCardDescription: Project description written by the workspace owner.
detail.neighborsCardDescription: Other projects directly depended on or referenced.
detail.neighborsMoreNote: You can browse all {count} connections in the connection map section of the main content.
detail.neighborsEmpty: No links to other projects yet. Add a project slug to `dependencies:` in the frontmatter and it'll show up here.
detail.saveErrorConflict: This project file was changed first by another editor or AI agent. Refresh and save again.

editor.confirmDiscardChanges: You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
editor.loadErrorDuplicate: The project to duplicate could not be found.
editor.vaultConflict: This project file was changed first by another editor or AI agent. Refresh and save again.
editor.createdAndOpenToast: "{name}" created · fill in details on the edit screen
editor.headerSubtitle: An edit screen that organizes metadata, connections, screenshots, and detailed descriptions in one place.
editor.openPublicAria: Open the public view in a new tab
editor.savedNotice: Just saved. You can keep refining here, or open the public view to verify the result immediately.
editor.tipTitleEasiest: Fill in name, category, status, and a short description, then save first.
editor.tipDescEasiest: Reopen later to enrich connections, screenshots, and operational info gradually.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep1: 1. The slug auto-fills as you type the name.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep2: 2. A single short description fills out the public card and drawer.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep3: 3. Detail fields can be expanded below and filled in as needed.
editor.publicCompanionDesc: Even while editing, you can open the public detail and linked docs to see the actual published state. Changes appear immediately on the public view after saving and reopening.

header.title: Docs Vault
header.topologyTooltip: Open the graph view for this vault

vaultStatus.localTooltip: Pick a folder on your disk as the vault
vaultStatus.unsupportedTooltip: Local folders need Chrome, Edge, or Safari 18.2+ (File System Access API)
vaultStatus.permissionNeededBanner: Vault is paused — re-grant folder access to keep editing. Showing sample docs until then.
vaultStatus.errorBanner: Vault folder is unavailable ({message}). Showing sample docs — open the picker to re-pick or refresh.

advanced.ontologyHintPrefix: First time? Try selecting this repo's
advanced.ontologyHintSuffix: — this tool's own ontology vault appears in the tree instantly (dogfooding).

topology.emptyBody: Auto-generate the Folder-Topology file structure (projects/_.md, categories.md, statuses.md) and the topology will show up. Existing files are not overwritten.
topology.scaffoldCta: Initialize this folder as a topology vault

commands.scaffoldTopology: Initialize this folder as a topology vault

dialog.deleteConfirm:
Really delete?

{title}
{slug}.md

This cannot be undone.
dialog.scaffoldConfirm:
Initialize this folder as a Folder-Topology vault?

- projects/ directory + 2 sample projects
- categories.md · statuses.md default sets
- README.md

Existing files are not overwritten.
dialog.scaffoldDone:
Initialization complete — {created} new files · {skipped} skipped

For field specs, open the freshly generated README.md first. Use the 2 sample projects (sample-hub / sample-leaf) frontmatter as a template.
dialog.noHeadings: This document has no h2/h3 headings.
dialog.notLocalFile: This document is not a local vault file.
dialog.vaultConflict: This vault file was modified externally (other editor / AI). Refresh and save again.
dialog.notRendered: Document is not rendered yet. Try again.
dialog.importOverwriteConfirm:
{count} documents already exist in the vault.

Yes = Overwrite (saveDoc)
No = Skip duplicates

Proceed?

topology.selectionAnnouncement: {name} selected. {deps, plural, one {# dependency} other {# dependencies}}, {referenced, plural, one {# reference} other {# references}}.

errorFallback.title: Topology failed to render
errorFallback.body: WebGL context may have been lost or there's a GPU resource issue. Try again or switch to the tree view.

workspace.subtitle: Workspace · {projects} projects · {hubs} hubs{growth}
workspace.description: {hubs} hubs are at the heart of {projects} projects. Click a node to open its details.

empty.noMatchesBody: No projects match the current filters.
empty.kicker: TOPOLOGY · {count, plural, one {# NODE} other {# NODES}}
empty.bodyNoProjects: Connect a markdown folder or create your first project in the builder. Fill in `dependencies:` in the frontmatter and lines appear here automatically.
empty.bodyNoDeps: Add a single dependency between two projects (`dependencies:` frontmatter) and a line will appear here automatically. The builder canvas can also draw them via drag.
empty.crossViewHint: This view only shows *project dependencies*. Other node kinds — domains, capabilities, elements — live in the tree and the builder.
empty.ctaBuilder: Add a dependency in the builder

hint.title: Ontology terrain map
hint.body: Nodes are projects · domains · capabilities · elements. Lines are depends-on / related / contains. Click to open details, search to narrow.

ontologyView.title: Ontology tree

titleTooltip.heading: Hierarchical view of ontology nodes
titleTooltip.body: A tree grown directly from the frontmatter of `.md` files in your vault. No review step — changes apply instantly. Expanded in the order <hierarchy>project → domain → capability → element</hierarchy>.
titleTooltip.footer: Document nodes act as evidence and are excluded from the tree. To draw the graph yourself, use the ‘Open builder’ button on the right.

ontologyView.titleTooltipAria: Explain what the ontology tree is

actions.globalSearchAria: Open global search across nodes and projects
actions.builderTooltip: Builder canvas — pick a kind from the palette on the left, drag handles to add relations
actions.builderAria: Ontology builder — draw directly on the canvas

stat.warningsHint: Relations dropped from the tree (multi-parent etc.)

ontologyView.error: Something went wrong while loading ontology data. {message}
ontologyView.emptyHint: The ontology hasn't grown yet. Follow the steps below to start your first graph.

getStarted.headingLocal: Vault is empty — write frontmatter and it grows instantly
getStarted.headingDefault: The tree grows as documents grow
getStarted.bodyLocal: There are 0 `.md` files with ontology frontmatter in the active vault. Take the next 2 steps to create your first node.
getStarted.bodyDefault: An ontology is a graph of concepts and relations from vault frontmatter. Take the next 3 steps to grow your first tree.
getStarted.stepLocalFrontmatterDesc: Add frontmatter like `kind: capability` to any `.md` file in the vault and a stub node appears immediately.
getStarted.stepLocalBuilderDesc: Use the /ontology/edit canvas to visually polish nodes and relations — saves to the vault and updates the tree.
getStarted.stepStaticVaultDesc: From /docs, pick a markdown folder to activate the vault.
getStarted.stepStaticFrontmatterDesc: Write kind / capabilities / elements / relates in your docs and stub nodes are created automatically.
getStarted.stepStaticBuilderDesc: Polish nodes and relations on the /ontology/edit canvas and they grow right into this tree.
getStarted.snippetSummary: Your vault's first node — paste this frontmatter into a `.md` file and it appears in the tree right away
getStarted.snippetHelp: Save the content above into your vault as something like capabilities/auth.md. More frontmatter keys: domain · capabilities · elements · relates · dependencies.

footer.countsHint: Includes implicit stubs declared in frontmatter — may exceed the count of .md files in the vault.

copyLink.tooltip: Copy a direct link to this node

detail.egoTwoHopTooltip: Includes neighbors one step further — labels may overlap with more nodes
detail.neighborMissingTitle: A node with this ID isn't in the graph yet
detail.stubWarning: Unresolved reference — the slug pointed to by frontmatter doesn't exist in the vault. Create a node with this name in the builder (/ontology/edit), or remove the reference from frontmatter.

localVaultPicker.unsupported: This browser doesn't support the File System Access API, so the local vault is unavailable. Use Chrome, Edge, Safari 18.2+, or Opera.
localVaultPicker.permissionNeeded: We have a previously opened folder. Re-authorize to keep using it.
localVaultPicker.errorFallback: Local vault error — there was a problem accessing the folder.
localVaultPicker.errorHint: Re-check folder permission or try a different folder.
localVaultPicker.validationTooltip: {errors, plural, =0 {{warnings} frontmatter warning(s) — run pnpm vault:validate for detail} other {{errors} frontmatter error(s){warnings, plural, =0 {} other {, {warnings} warning(s)}} — some nodes are missing from the graph. Run pnpm vault:validate for detail}}

starterCta.emptyBodyLine1: Creates 5 starter markdown files (project / domain / capability / element + README) and an .mcp.json.example for AI agent registration in this folder.
starterCta.emptyBodyLine2: Edit the frontmatter to match your project and it shows up in the tree, topology, and builder right away.
starterCta.secondaryTitle: Adds 5 ontology starter files (existing files are left untouched)

vaultStubs.emptyBody: No `.md` in your vault has frontmatter `kind:` yet, so the ontology is empty. Add `kind: project` (or capability / element / workflow / decision) to a doc's frontmatter and a node appears immediately.
vaultStubs.intro: Frontmatter `kind:` in your vault's `.md` files becomes nodes directly — no extraction or review step.
vaultStubs.polishSummary: Want to polish nodes and relations visually?
vaultStubs.polishBody: Open the same vault in the `/ontology/edit/` builder canvas as an ERD-like view and edit node positions / frontmatter keys inline. Saves go directly back to the vault `.md` files — no separate promote step.

sections.basicsDescription: Settle the name, slug, category, and status.
sections.basicsDetailedDescription: First align the name, identifier, and current category and status.
sections.storyDescription: Enter description, markdown body, tags, and links.
sections.storyDetailedDescription: Fill the description shown on node cards, drawers, and the detail page.
sections.networkDetailedDescription: Review dependencies and screen materials together.
sections.operationsDescription: Wrap up timeline, owner, icon, and hub flag.
sections.operationsDetailedDescription: Finish operational signals like timeline, owner, and hub flag.

fields.name: Name
fields.slugHint: Used in URLs. Letters, digits, and hyphens only. Cannot change after creation.
fields.duplicateNotice: Starting from a copy of the existing project.
fields.categoryMissingWarning: Current category reference is broken. Pick another category to recover.
fields.statusMissingWarning: Current status reference is broken. Pick another status to recover.
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: 1-2 line summary — shown in drawer and node
fields.detailPlaceholder:
# Heading

**Markdown supported** — shown on detail page
fields.dependenciesHint: Pick the hubs or upstream projects this one connects to.

validation.globalErrorBanner: There are items to fix before saving.
validation.globalErrorMore: + {count} more. Check each in the fields below.

actions.headerHelpCreate: Save first; you can fill the rest later.
actions.createNoticeStay: Project created — ready to keep editing.
actions.editNoticeStay: Saved the changes. You can verify on the right preview and the public link.

tip.lead: Just enter name, status, and a short description.
tip.step1: 1. Entering a name auto-generates the slug.
tip.step3: 3. Even a single-line description shows up in cards and drawers.
tip.step4: 4. Add connections and ops info later by reopening.

preview.liveHint: Inputs on the left reflect here and on public screens immediately.
preview.noChanges: Same as saved. Move on to compare against the public screen.
preview.cardCaption: How it actually renders in the topology.
preview.changeCategoryStatus: Category and status will look like "{category} / {status}".
preview.changeDescriptionFilled: Short description will reflect on cards and drawers immediately.
preview.changeDescriptionEmpty: Short description is empty, so card and drawer summaries shrink.
preview.changeDetailFilled: Detail body will be shown on the public detail page.
preview.changeDetailEmpty: Detail body is empty, so the public detail page is simplified.

dependencyPicker.emptyHint: No dependencies selected. Pick from below to add.
dependencyPicker.suggestionsHeading: Suggestion — other projects found in the description
dependencyPicker.missingHint: These project references no longer exist. Click chips to remove them one by one.

quickCreate.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this project does in one line.
quickCreate.errorRequired: Please enter a project name and one-line description first.

quickEdit.headerSubtitle: Change name and description without breaking your reading flow.
quickEdit.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this project in one line…
quickEdit.errorEmpty: Name and one-line description cannot be empty.

publicQuickActions.infoHintBody: Attach docs that explain this project, or edit project info in detail.
publicQuickActions.infoNewBody: Add a new project to the workspace you are in.
publicQuickActions.infoEditBody: Edit project details — status, tags, links, and more.
